mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy that reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts signals weak demand for residential construction, which correlates with lower consumer confidence and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in housing contraction periods exceed 10% or Rank IC > 0.05 in corresponding windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and decline in unemployment suggests strong supply-side capacity utilization and labor market tightness, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in co-occurring INDPRO/UNRATE regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2% erodes real returns and forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation periods exceed 5% or Rank IC > 0.05 in corresponding windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with weak housing starts (HOUST) indicates deteriorating consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in co-occurring RSAFS/HOUST declines exceed 10% or Rank IC > 0.05 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 5% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-unemployment periods exceed 5% or Rank IC < 0.03 in corresponding windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in GDP growth combined with stable inflation indicates strong economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in co-occurring GDP/inflation regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03 in 3-year rolling windows.
